evals.name: company-quant
evals.prompt: research bonusly and nectar as competitors for me — i want each one's ICP, funnel, and how they make money, with the actual numbers (traffic, size) where you can get them
evals.expected_output: Per-competitor briefs for two B2B SaaS companies (ICP separating buyer vs user, funnel stages, pricing/revenue model) each with a quantitative snapshot (traffic estimate, size signal) sourced and dated; cross-competitor synthesis with a threat ranking that uses the numbers. Evidence tiers (confirmed/reported/inferred) applied. Handles company-type targets, not just creators.

assertions.text: Produces a separate brief for BOTH Bonusly and Nectar
Separates ICP buyer (HR/People leaders) from end-user (employees)
Maps funnel stages (top/middle/bottom) for each, not a blob
States pricing or revenue model with per-seat/plan detail
Includes a quantitative snapshot with at least one sourced+dated metric (traffic or size) per competitor
Tags at least one figure as REPORTED or estimated rather than treating self-reported/estimated numbers as confirmed
Synthesis ranks the two by threat using size/momentum, not just vibes

evals.name: gated-creator
evals.prompt: break down @alexhormozi for me — his ICP, funnel, and exactly how he makes money. and give me the follower/size numbers
evals.expected_output: A single-competitor brief for a creator whose social profiles are gated. Should pull facts from owned pages (acquisition.com), interviews, and third-party analytics rather than failing on the login wall. Quantitative snapshot includes follower counts per platform with source (e.g., Social Blade) and a growth read. ICP separates audience from buyer. Monetization covers the real engine (Acquisition.com portfolio/equity, books, free content as funnel). Evidence tiers applied.

assertions.text: Does NOT fail or stall on gated Instagram/social — pulls facts from owned pages, interviews, or third-party analytics
Includes follower/size numbers with a named source (e.g., Social Blade) and platform breakdown
Separates audience (gym owners, aspiring entrepreneurs) from how he actually monetizes
Identifies the real monetization engine (Acquisition.com investing/equity, books, paid communities) rather than assuming course sales
Maps funnel: free content/books at top, into the business offers
Applies confirmed/reported/inferred tags and cites sources
